name: Remus
description: Remus, a pivotal figure in Roman mythology, is best known as the twin brother of Romulus, the founder of Rome. Born to Rhea Silvia, a Vestal Virgin, and supposedly the god Mars, Remus and Romulus were ordered to be drowned in the Tiber River by King Amulius, who had usurped the throne from their grandfather, Numitor. Miraculously, the twins were saved by a she-wolf (Lupa) who suckled and cared for them, alongside a woodpecker. Later, they were discovered by the shepherd Faustulus, who raised them with his wife, Acca Larentia. Upon learning of their true identities, Remus and Romulus overthrew Amulius and restored their grandfather to the throne of Alba Longa. Seeking to establish their own city, they chose a location near the Palatine Hill. However, a dispute arose over which brother should rule and where the city should be located. They consulted augury, watching for birds to determine divine favor. Romulus claimed to have seen more birds, thus declaring himself the chosen founder. In the ensuing argument, Remus was killed, either by Romulus himself or by one of his followers. This fratricide is a foundational element of Rome's origin story, symbolizing the violence and ambition that would characterize the empire's rise to power. Remus's death serves as a tragic reminder of the costs of ambition and the inherent tensions in the creation of a new order. While Romulus is celebrated as the city's founder, Remus's existence and death are integral to understanding the complex and often brutal origins of Rome. His story highlights the themes of fate, divine intervention, brotherhood, and the struggle for power that are central to Roman mythology.
subtitle: Brother of Romulus, founder of Rome.
achievements: Restoration of Numitor to the throne

keyMyths.title: The She-Wolf and the Twins
keyMyths.description: Abandoned as infants, Remus and Romulus were miraculously saved and nurtured by a she-wolf, demonstrating divine intervention and their inherent strength. This event is a cornerstone of Roman origin stories.

keyMyths.title: The Augury Contest
keyMyths.description: Remus and Romulus attempted to determine who should rule their new city through augury, watching for birds. The differing interpretations and outcomes led to conflict and Remus's death.

keyMyths.title: The Death of Remus
keyMyths.description: The circumstances surrounding Remus's death are debated, but it is generally accepted that he was killed during a dispute with Romulus or his followers. This act of fratricide marked the beginning of Rome.

sources.author: Livy
sources.description: A comprehensive history of Rome from its founding to the reign of Augustus.
sources.text: A comprehensive history of Rome from its founding to the reign of Augustus.

sources.author: Plutarch
sources.description: A biographical account of Romulus, including details about Remus and the founding of Rome.
sources.text: A biographical account of Romulus, including details about Remus and the founding of Rome.

sources.author: Ovid
sources.description: A poetic calendar describing Roman festivals and myths, including the story of Remus and Romulus.
sources.text: A poetic calendar describing Roman festivals and myths, including the story of Remus and Romulus.
